message.content:
[Wed 2026-04-08 16:28 EDT] Quarterly security content update task in workspace /Users/louishyman/.openclaw/workspaces/worf-workspace.

Goal: refresh SECURITY-BRIEF.md with material new personal AI agent security threats from the last quarter (relative to current date 2026-04-08), and update mitigations.

Instructions:
1) Locate and read SECURITY-BRIEF.md.
2) Research credible recent developments (roughly last 3 months) affecting personal AI agents / local agent runtimes / tool-using assistants (prompt-injection evolutions, MCP/plugin/tool abuse patterns, supply chain compromises, auth/session risks, messaging channel abuse, browser automation abuse, local privilege escalation vectors, model fallback/sandboxing lessons, etc.).
3) Keep only material changes; avoid rehashing old baseline items unless updated by new evidence.
4) Edit SECURITY-BRIEF.md in place with:
   - “What changed this quarter” section
   - Updated threat list with severity and relevance to this setup
   - Concrete recommended controls (practical, prioritized)
   - Date stamp for this quarterly refresh
5) Preserve readability and concise executive-summary tone.
6) At the end, provide a brief changelog summary in your final response.

Constraints:
- Use trustworthy sources and avoid speculation.
- If uncertain on a claim, label it as low-confidence or omit.
- Make changes directly in file and then report what changed.

I’m collecting one more confirmed sandbox/runtime item and then I’ll draft the exact quarterly section content.I found the security brief path now and will read it next, then draft an in-place quarterly rewrite aligned to its existing format.I’ve finished collecting primary-source advisories; I’m extracting exact dates/severities/affected versions now so the brief only includes defensible, source-backed changes.I’m ready to patch `SECURITY-BRIEF.md` with a concrete, source-backed quarterly update. I’ll attempt an in-place edit now; if filesystem policy blocks writes, I’ll provide the exact replacement content for you to apply.Write access is blocked for this path from the current session (`outside session root` + read-only FS), so I can’t modify `SECURITY-BRIEF.md` in place. I’ve prepared a complete source-backed replacement section set below and will include a concise changelog so you can apply it directly.I could not edit [SECURITY-BRIEF.md](/Users/louishyman/.openclaw/workspaces/worf-workspace/SECURITY-BRIEF.md) in place from this session because the path is outside the writable session root and the filesystem is read-only.  
Below is a ready-to-paste replacement that satisfies your requested quarterly refresh.

```md
# SECURITY-BRIEF

_Last updated: 2026-04-08 (quarterly refresh; coverage window: 2026-01-08 to 2026-04-08)_

## Executive Summary
The material shift this quarter is a run of **confirmed RCE-class flaws in agent runtimes and tool connectors**, not just theoretical jailbreak risk. One Langflow issue (CVE-2026-33017) is now in CISA KEV, indicating active exploitation. For this setup, the top priority is strict tool isolation: patch quickly, force sandboxing on all execution paths (including fallback models), and keep all agent endpoints non-public.

## What Changed This Quarter
**Refresh date:** 2026-04-08

1. **Langflow unauthenticated code injection reached KEV status** (Critical)
- CVE-2026-33017 / GHSA-vwmf-pq79-vjvx, published March 2026, affects `<=1.8.2`, fixed in `1.9.0`.
- NVD records it as CISA KEV-listed (added 2026-03-25).

2. **Authenticated assistant flows still enabled model-to-code execution** (Critical)
- CVE-2026-33873 / GHSA-v8hw-mh8c-jxfc, published March 2026, affects `<=1.8.1`, fixed in `1.9.0`.
- Shows that “auth required” does not eliminate practical RCE risk in agentic assistants.

3. **Prompt-injection-to-code-execution pattern was reaffirmed** (High)
- CVE-2026-27966 / GHSA-3645-fxcv-hqr4, published 2026-02-25, affects `<1.6.9`, fixed in `1.8.0`.
- CSV agent path allowed dangerous Python execution under attacker-controlled prompt/data conditions.

4. **MCP connector boundary failures became concrete** (High)
- CVE-2026-27735 / GHSA-vjqx-cfc4-9h6v in `mcp-server-git`, published 2026-02-25, affects `<=2026.1.13`, fixed in `2026.1.14`.
- Path validation bypass allows staging files outside intended repo root.

5. **“Sandboxed” local runtime escape was demonstrated** (High)
- CVE-2026-27597 / GHSA-f229-3862-4942 in Enclave, published 2026-02-24, affects `<=2.11.0`, fixed in `2.11.1`.
- Confirms language-level sandboxing alone is insufficient for hostile input.

## Updated Threat List (Severity + Relevance Here)
1. **Internet-exposed agent endpoints leading to RCE** — Critical  
Relevance: any non-loopback exposure of local agent/gateway interfaces creates high-impact compromise risk.

2. **Authenticated session abuse + model-generated code execution** — Critical  
Relevance: browser/message-originated prompt injection or stolen sessions can trigger code-exec paths in tool-using assistants.

3. **MCP/tool connector filesystem boundary bypass** — High  
Relevance: local file/git tools can be coerced beyond intended workspace scope without strict canonicalization/allowlists.

4. **Sandbox escape in local execution runtimes** — High  
Relevance: fallback/small-model workflows that execute code with weak isolation can become host compromise paths.

5. **Patch-lag in fast-moving agent dependencies** — High  
Relevance: multiple critical disclosures in one quarter make stale versions an immediate, practical risk.

## Recommended Controls (Practical, Prioritized)
**P0 (24–48h)**
- Keep all assistant/runtime endpoints loopback-only unless explicitly required.
- Patch if present:
  - Langflow `>=1.9.0` (also review CSV Agent behavior per CVE-2026-27966).
  - `mcp-server-git >=2026.1.14`.
  - Enclave `>=2.11.1`.
- Enforce sandboxing for all model paths, including fallback models.
- Deny high-impact tools (shell/browser/file-write/networked tools) on fallback or untrusted-input paths by default.

**P1 (This week)**
- Run tool-executing runtime under a dedicated least-privilege OS account.
- Shorten session/token lifetimes; require re-auth for high-impact actions.
- Isolate browser automation profile from personal logged-in profile (no shared cookies/password store).
- Lock down secret directories and re-check host firewall posture.

**P2 (This cycle)**
- Add dependency/CVE monitoring for agent stack components with critical patch SLA.
- Add regression tests for path confinement and deny-by-default tool routing.
- Re-run security posture review monthly and on any new RCE advisory.

## Confidence
- Confidence level: **High** (all items from official GitHub Security Advisories and/or NVD; KEV status from NVD/CISA integration).
- Excluded: unverified social/media reports and speculative attack narratives.

## Tracking
- Next quarterly refresh target: **2026-07-01 to 2026-07-15**.
- Trigger out-of-band update on any new KEV-listed agent/tool CVE or MCP auth/boundary bypass advisory.

## Primary Sources
- https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-33017
- https://github.com/langflow-ai/langflow/security/advisories/GHSA-vwmf-pq79-vjvx
- https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-v8hw-mh8c-jxfc
- https://github.com/langflow-ai/langflow/security/advisories/GHSA-3645-fxcv-hqr4
- https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers/security/advisories/GHSA-vjqx-cfc4-9h6v
- https://github.com/agentfront/enclave/security/advisories/GHSA-f229-3862-4942
```

Changelog summary:
1. Replaced generic quarterly statements with five source-backed Q1 2026 threat changes.
2. Updated threat list to include severity plus direct relevance to local/personal agent runtime setups.
3. Re-prioritized controls into `P0/P1/P2` with concrete version and boundary-hardening actions.
4. Added explicit refresh date/window, confidence note, and primary-source citations.
